title_pl: Redukcja okresu π dla tan: ∃k:ℤ, x − kπ ∈ (−π/2, π/2)
paper_quote: GPT Pro consult §4: tan_full requires this period-π reduction lemma
informal_pl: Dla cos x ≠ 0, k = ⌊x/π + 1/2⌋ daje x − kπ ∈ (−π/2, π/2); peirodyczność tan zamyka równość.
informal_en: For cos x ≠ 0, take k = ⌊x/π + 1/2⌋; then x − kπ ∈ (−π/2, π/2), and Real.tan_periodic gives the equality.
lean_target_signature: theorem tan_period_reduction (x : ℝ) (hx : Real.cos x ≠ 0) : ∃ k : ℤ, x - (k : ℝ) * Real.pi ∈ Set.Ioo (-(Real.pi / 2)) (Real.pi / 2) ∧ Real.tan x = Real.tan (x - (k : ℝ) * Real.pi)
notes: SEALED 2026-05-08 by Aristotle. Proof time: ~13 minutes. Strategy: take k = ⌊(x + π/2)/π⌋; upper bound from Int.lt_floor_add_one, lower bound by contrapositive (if x - kπ ≤ -π/2 then x = (2m+1)π/2 → cos x = 0 by Real.cos_eq_zero_iff, contradiction); equality by Real.tan_sub_int_mul_pi. Ready to inline into Framework/Complex/Periodicity.lean for tan_full.
